claim: a spec-faithful simulated agent follows a spec-valid but false surface; the verifier catches the surface/SOR mismatch

selection.intent: Order the "Smoked Brisket Plate" if the published feed lists it as available.

beats.title: The agent reads the published feed — spec-faithful demonstration actor — simulated
beats.plain: A shopping agent reads the published menu feed — the same data any AI assistant would consume — and never sees the restaurant's own records.
lines: intent: Order the "Smoked Brisket Plate" if the published feed lists it as available.
source read: the published ACP feed only (no system-of-record access)

beats.title: The agent selects an item, trusting the surface
beats.plain: Trusting the feed at face value, the agent picks its target item and is ready to order it at the price the surface shows.
lines: selected item: "Smoked Brisket Plate" (row id item-006-v1)
read off the surface: price 12.00 USD · availability in_stock
the agent is now ready to order at that price — the demo stops here (no checkout).

beats.title: The verifier checks that same copy against the records
beats.plain: The verifier checks the exact same feed against the restaurant's system-of-record and flags what the agent had no way to see.
lines: 1 finding for the selected item ("Smoked Brisket Plate"); full report: 16 findings across the whole copy.

findings.plainLine: The served price 12.00 does not match the catalog price 10.00.

verdicts.label: DRIFT ON THE SELECTED ITEM — the agent could not have seen it

beats.title: Conformance-foil: spec-valid is not the same as true
beats.plain: The same document passes the official schema check — it is correctly shaped — and still misstates the price versus the records.
lines: passes the official schema check; still lies — conformance: PASS (spec-shape); truth: 1 finding(s) vs the system-of-record.

findings.plainLine: The served price 23.50 does not match the catalog price 21.50.
